clusters.0: clever protagonists earning moral authority through pragmatic problem-solving
clusters.1: privileged witnesses confront moral hypocrisy exposed through proximity and crisis
clusters.2: protagonist grapples with divided loyalty across cultural or class boundaries

clusters.9: ambitious protagonists reassessing success through mentorship, rivalry, or self-reflection

facets.protagonist: idealistic revolutionary group gradually corrupted by power
facets.relationship: collective uprising against oppressive authority
facets.arc: disillusionment as liberators become indistinguishable from former oppressors
